title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Runtime Contract: Production Protocol Defense
summary: A release claims HTTP/3 because UDP is open, ignores QUIC connection identity and migration, treats QPACK like HPACK, advertises Alt-Svc without rollback, and cannot defend parser, TLS, proxy, or capacity evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production protocol defense increment in multi-version protocol release and incident defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.
Stage Alt-Svc discovery, certificate and authority checks, version negotiation, fallback, cache lifetime, disablement, and rollback without breaking HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Production Protocol Defense · Theory Production Protocol Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 7858 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Protocol Defense contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Production Protocol Defense · Theory Production Protocol Defense.

For Predict Production Protocol Defense · Theory Production Protocol Defense, keep Production Protocol Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Production Protocol Defense evidence · Theory Production Protocol Defense
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
